name: Crohn's Disease - Fecal Calprotectin Lab Query (Elevated)
description: Tests ability to find Crohn's patients via elevated fecal calprotectin. Calprotectin is a neutrophil-derived protein elevated in active intestinal inflammation. The clinical cutoff for active IBD is typically >250 mcg/g (some labs use >150). The Synthea module generates calprotectin >500 mcg/g for active Crohn's patients.
prompt: Find patients with elevated fecal calprotectin lab results greater than 250 micrograms per gram of stool — the clinical threshold suggesting active inflammatory bowel disease.

prompts.broad: Find patients with elevated fecal calprotectin (a stool marker of intestinal inflammation) — values greater than 250 mcg/g of stool, suggesting active IBD.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources with elevated fecal calprotectin. Use LOINC code 38445-3 (Calprotectin [Mass/mass] in Stool). Filter by value-quantity greater than 250 mcg/g. Query: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|38445-3&value-quantity=gt250||ug/g

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with elevated fecal calprotectin suggesting active IBD. Sensitive but used to differentiate IBD from IBS.
